narrative:
Nicolas Pépé is a right winger representing France at the World Cup. His overall rating of 7.11/10 places him in the top 11% of right wingers at this tournament. This rating is significantly influenced by his club form over the last two seasons.

Pépé demonstrates strong offensive output at this World Cup, scoring 0.738 goals per 90 minutes and providing 0.369 assists per 90 minutes. His 1.475 key passes per 90 minutes highlight his creative contribution to the attack. Defensively, he records only 0.369 tackles per 90 minutes, indicating a more attack-focused role for his team.

Pépé appears to be a crucial attacking asset for France, having played 244 minutes and contributing significantly to their offensive play. Scouts and fans should watch for his continued ability to convert chances, as his goals per 90 min (0.738) currently exceed his expected goals per 90 min (0.427). His defensive involvement, with only 0.369 tackles per 90, also bears watching in upcoming fixtures.
